description_long: This is a weathered wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by three dark, metallic hoops—one at the top, one at the middle, and one at the bottom. The wood appears aged and stained, with a rough, matte finish and visible grain patterns, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements. The top of the barrel is flat and fully enclosed, indicating the presence of a fitted wooden lid. There are no visible handles or spouts, and the surface shows no labels, engravings, or painted markings. The metallic hoops are slightly corroded, adding to the rustic, utilitarian appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in outdoor urban environments, such as alleys, storage yards, or near garages, where it might be used for storage or as a decorative element.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with dark metal hoops and a flat, enclosed top, showing signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full cylindrical body, aged wood texture, and all three metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated, this view highlights the curvature of the staves and the weathered wood grain.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the middle and lower metal hoops and the darker wood coloration.
description_view_3: Another side view, showing the flat top lid and the consistent weathered appearance of the wood and metal.
